Consolidate file path resolution for WebView controls (#34620)

<!-- Please let the below note in for people that find this PR -->
> [!NOTE]
> Are you waiting for the changes in this PR to be merged?
> It would be very helpful if you could [test the resulting
artifacts](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Testing-PR-Builds) from
this PR and let us know in a comment if this change resolves your issue.
Thank you!

## Description

Refactored scattered file-path resolution logic in HybridWebView and
BlazorWebView into shared utilities (`FileSystemUtils.Combine`,
`WebUtils.ResolveRelativePath`) to fix edge cases where `Path.Combine`
drops the root directory when a relative path starts with a separator.

### Changes

- **`FileSystemUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `IsValidRelativePath()` and
`Combine()` methods that validate relative paths and ensure the resolved
path stays within the expected root directory
- **`WebUtils.shared.cs`** — Added `ResolveRelativePath()` to resolve
request URIs against an app origin into validated relative paths
- **HybridWebView handlers** (iOS, Windows, Android) — Use the new
shared utilities for file path resolution
- **BlazorWebView file providers** (iOS, Android, Windows, Tizen) — Use
`FileSystemUtils.Combine()` for path resolution
- **`Email.windows.cs`** — Consolidated duplicate `NormalizePath` into
`FileSystemUtils.NormalizePath`
- **Device tests** — Added path resolution tests for both HybridWebView
and BlazorWebView covering relative paths, rooted paths, dot-dot
segments, and encoded separators

commit.message:
Fix sprite batch lighting bounds accumulation

Box3_Union was accumulating in->bounds (the first sprite) instead of
batch->bounds (the current sprite), so R_ActiveLights only considered
the first sprite's position for the entire batch. When that sprite was
far from an explosion, the lighting query found no lights, driving
diffuse to zero and making lit sprites invisible under additive
blending. Since particle order varies per frame, this caused flickering.

commit.message:
fix: prevent 304 bypass of body rewriting via conditional request caching

Two related bugs fixed:

1. Outbound conditional request headers not stripped
   The proxy was forwarding If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Match,
   If-Unmodified-Since, and If-Range to the upstream unchanged.  When the
   browser had a cached copy from a previous proxy run and resent these headers,
   the upstream could return 304 Not Modified.  ModifyResponse's noBody path
   skips all body rewriting on 304 responses, so the browser would then display
   its stale cached copy — which may contain unrewritten upstream hostnames.
   Fix: strip all five conditional headers in the Director before forwarding.

2. ETag + Last-Modified response headers not stripped
   By caching these validators the browser would eventually send conditional
   requests (see bug 1).  Strip both in headersStrip so the browser never has
   a validator to send for proxied resources.

Add TestConditionalRequestHeadersStripped and TestETagLastModifiedStripped.

commit.message:
feat(acp): redesign streaming reveal flow (#107)

## Summary

Replace the markdown streaming path with a dedicated reveal engine so
assistant text renders as a controlled character-clock reveal instead of
raw batch bursts.

## What changed

- introduced a pure `StreamingRevealEngine` plus a Svelte controller to
decouple canonical streamed text from displayed reveal state
- rebuilt `markdown-text.svelte` around revealed text, incremental tail
parsing, and stable settled/live section handling
- fixed remount recovery, grapheme-safe reveal, trailing cursor
behavior, and post-stream follow behavior while a thinking row trails
the thread
- threaded reveal keys through the assistant message text path so
resumed streaming entries can recover without replaying fresh streams
from blank

## Why this approach

The old renderer mixed transport timing, markdown parsing, and view
updates in one place, which made streaming feel visibly jumpy and
fragile around virtualization and async markdown handoff. This change
gives the stream a small timing core, keeps render optimizations local
to markdown, and lets follow behavior stay aligned with the actual
latest target.

## Validation

- targeted Bun tests for the reveal engine, reveal controller, and
thread-follow behavior
- targeted Vitest coverage for incremental streaming-tail parsing and
markdown streaming integration
- `bun run check` is still blocked by unrelated pre-existing TypeScript
errors in the modified-files / tool-call-edit area of the repo baseline

---

Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
🤖 Generated with GPT-5.4 via [GitHub Copilot
CLI](https://github.com/features/copilot)

---
## Summary by cubic
Redesigned assistant message streaming to use a paced reveal engine with
a blinking cursor, replacing bursty markdown updates. Streaming is
smoother and more stable, with accurate scroll-follow and safer remount
recovery.

- **New Features**
- Added `StreamingRevealEngine` and `createStreamingReveal` for
grapheme-safe reveal with pause and catch‑up modes.
- Rebuilt `markdown-text.svelte` around incremental tail parsing via
`parseStreamingTailIncremental`; keeps settled blocks stable, shows a
blinking cursor, and defers rich markdown and badge mounts until
streaming ends.
- Threaded `revealMessageKey` from both `virtual-session-list` and
`virtualized-entry-list` into `assistant-message` →
`content-block-router` → `text-block` → `markdown-text`, namespaced per
thought/message group to seed reveal across remounts; added tests for
the engine, controller, and incremental parser.

- **Bug Fixes**
- Virtualization always observes the latest reveal target (even when not
streaming); `message-wrapper` uses `requestLatestReveal` to keep
scroll-follow aligned.
- Grapheme-safe advancing prevents emoji splits and clamps large frame
gaps to avoid over-reveal.
- Aligned modified-file diff handling with `@pierre/diffs`: use
`newLines`/`oldLines`, treat per-hunk additions/deletions as arrays, and
fix revert/annotation logic to stay correct when metadata is partial.

commit.message:
Fix scheduled task leaving stale segments after analysis (#734)

Remove pushToJellyfin parameter from BaseItemAnalyzerTask.AnalyzeItemsAsync and
the UpdateMediaSegments config gate from the analysis push path.

Previously, DetectSegmentsTask passed pushToJellyfin: false and relied on
Jellyfin's TaskExtractMediaSegments to pull updated segments. This approach
could silently fail if the task wasn't found or wasn't idle, leaving users with
stale segments after analysis.

Now all callers (scheduled task, automatic task, ScanSeason) push newly-analyzed
segments directly via UpdateMediaSegmentsAsync immediately after analysis,
consistent with how the dashboard's ScanSeason option works. The Jellyfin
extraction task trigger is kept as an additional sync for already-analyzed items.

commit.message:
fix: skip body buffering for text/event-stream SSE responses

Server-Sent Events are long-lived streaming responses that must not pass
through io.ReadAll — doing so would block the client from receiving any
events until the stream closes (potentially never for persistent SSE).

Add an early-return in ModifyResponse when Content-Type is text/event-stream.
Header rewriting (host masking, CSP, etc.) still runs before the early exit,
so headers are correctly rewritten even for SSE responses.

FlushInterval=-1 on the ReverseProxy already handles progressive flushing;
this change ensures we don't inadvertently buffer the body first.

Add TestSSEPassthrough: spawns an upstream that writes one event then blocks
on a pipe; asserts the event is readable within 3 seconds (would time out if
the proxy was buffering the full stream).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading (#217)

* ⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading

The prior code used `ReadToEndAsync` to drain `stderr` when a Python worker process failed to return a valid response envelope. While this was wrapped in a cancellation token, `ReadToEndAsync` only cancels at internal read boundaries, meaning a process dumping megabytes of Python tracebacks would still cause large allocations and hang the application while the OS pipe buffer slowly flushed.

This change replaces `ReadToEndAsync` with a `ReadBoundedStderrAsync` method, which:
- Reads a maximum of 50 lines or 4096 characters using `ReadLineAsync` with strict `WaitAsync` boundaries.
- Silently drains the remainder of the pipe, allowing the OS pipe buffer to clear and ensuring the underlying subprocess can fully exit.
- Interpolates the previously unused `killSuffix` variable to accurately report when a kill operation was attempted before reading `stderr`.

This fix prevents hangs and OOM spikes when Python models crash.

commit.message:
fix(generator): align = operators in WHERE clause conditions (#49)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Aligns `=` operators in `WHERE` clause conditions so equality
comparisons are easy to scan vertically.

## Root Cause

`where_sql` delegated to `_inline_clause_sql`, which only handles the
leading-`AND` layout. It rendered each condition with natural spacing:

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (
    p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key
    OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL
  )
```

## Fix

`where_sql` now works at the AST level when the top-level is a pure
`AND` chain:

1. Flattens the `AND` chain with `_flatten_and`.
2. Collects all `EQ` nodes anywhere in the `WHERE` tree and computes
`max LHS width + 2` as the alignment target.
3. Pads top-level equality LHS to the target column.
4. Renders top-level `Paren` conditions compactly (single line) via
`_compact_sql`.

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame            = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key       = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL)
```

Falls back to `_inline_clause_sql` when the top-level condition is not
`AND` (e.g. a bare `OR`), preserving existing connector alignment
behaviour.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new `where_sql`, `_flatten_and`,
`_compact_sql`
- `tests/fixtures/select/where_eq_alignment.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture pair
- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/create_macro.expected.sql` — regenerated to
reflect aligned three-EQ WHERE
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — new "WHERE — `=` operator column
alignment" section

Resolves #48

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add offline docs.rs-style workspace docs check to Rust CI and fix surfaced rustdoc links (#1295)

Rust CI only validated docs for a single crate, which allowed docs.rs
regressions elsewhere in the workspace. This PR adds a workspace-wide
docs.rs simulation in offline mode so doc breakage is caught before
merge.

- **CI coverage: docs.rs simulation for the full Rust workspace**
- Added `rust::ci-docs-rs` to run a docs.rs-like build across all
workspace crates.
- Uses `DOCS_RS=1`, `--cfg docsrs`, nightly rustdoc, and offline doc
generation.

- **CI integration**
- Wired `ci-docs-rs` into `rust::ci-lint` so the check runs on standard
Rust CI paths.

- **Rustdoc correctness fixes uncovered by workspace-wide docs**
- Fixed broken/private intra-doc links across `mlt-core`, `mlt`,
`mlt-synthetics`, and `mlt-wasm`.
- Replaced invalid links to private or out-of-scope symbols with valid
public links or plain text where appropriate.

```just
ci-docs-rs:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    rustup toolchain install nightly --profile minimal
    export DOCS_RS=1
    export RUSTDOCFLAGS="${RUSTDOCFLAGS:+$RUSTDOCFLAGS } --cfg docsrs"
    cargo fetch --locked
    CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true cargo +nightly doc --workspace --no-deps --locked
```

fix: strip Report-To, NEL, COOP, COEP, CORP response headers

These headers were forwarded to the browser unchanged, causing two problems:

1. Report-To + NEL: instruct the browser to send network error/timing reports
   directly to upstream endpoints, leaking real hostnames and client traffic
   details even when the proxy successfully masks them from page content.

2. Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy (same-origin) + Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy
   (require-corp) enforce strict cross-origin isolation. In a proxy context
   this breaks subresource loading: COEP blocks resources that lack the
   correct CORP opt-in header, and COOP prevents cross-window communication
   across the proxied origin boundary.

3. Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy (same-origin) blocks the proxy from serving
   cross-origin subresource responses to the browser.

Add all six variants to headersStrip. Extend TestProxyCSPHSTSStripped to
assert all new headers are absent from proxy responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: deflate oversized body restoration was broken — remove inner LimitReader

When maxBodyBytes was set (e.g. -max-body 1), the deflate code was doing
io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxBodyBytes+1)), which read only a
partial compressed stream. The early-exit then restored those partial bytes
back to resp.Body, causing the client to receive truncated compressed data.

Fix: read the ENTIRE compressed body upfront (no inner size limit on the
compressed stream). The decompressed-size limit at the shared oversized-body
check (maxBodyBytes gate below) handles the limit correctly. When the
decompressed body exceeds maxBodyBytes, the oversized branch now restores
the complete compressedDeflate bytes and the Content-Encoding header.

Add TestDeflateOversizedBodyRestored to verify the fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix HTTP server failing to bind net_port

Sv_InitHttp runs during Sv_Init, before any map load triggers
Net_Config which creates the net_port cvar. Use Cvar_Add instead
of Cvar_Get so the cvar is created if it doesn't exist yet.

commit.message:
Merge User-Agent headers when cloning HTTP client (#383)

When `Client.clone()` was called with headers containing a `User-Agent`,
it would overwrite the base client's `User-Agent` rather than merging.
This loses the SDK's default UA token when callers supply their own.

## Changes

- **`packages/common/src/microsoft_teams/common/http/client.py`**
- Added `_merge_headers(base, overrides)` helper: performs a
case-insensitive `User-Agent` merge (space-concatenated, token-based
dedup to avoid substring false positives); all other headers follow
standard last-write-wins semantics
- `Client.clone()` now calls `_merge_headers()` instead of a plain dict
unpack

- **`packages/common/tests/test_client.py`**
- Added 7 tests covering: preservation without overrides, merge when
both sides define UA, non-UA overrides leave UA untouched,
case-insensitive key matching, duplicate-token suppression, substring
false-positive guard, and override-only UA when base has none

## Example

```python
client = Client(ClientOptions(headers={"User-Agent": "teams-py/1.0"}))
clone = client.clone(ClientOptions(headers={"User-Agent": "myapp/2.0"}))

# Before: "myapp/2.0"  (SDK UA lost)
# After:  "teams-py/1.0 myapp/2.0"
print(clone._options.headers["User-Agent"])
```

fix: deflate oversized-body corruption + add deflate to Accept-Encoding

Two related fixes to the deflate Content-Encoding support:

1. Oversized deflate body was corrupted on the round-trip back.
   When the decompressed deflate body exceeded maxBodyBytes, the code fell
   into the 'else' branch which called io.MultiReader(bytes.NewReader(raw),
   resp.Body), but resp.Body was already empty (consumed for zlib/flate
   detection) and 'raw' held decompressed bytes with Content-Encoding
   already stripped. The browser would receive partial decompressed data
   with no Content-Encoding — completely broken.

   Fix: hoist compressedDeflate to outer scope; in the oversized branch,
   restore resp.Body = bytes.NewReader(compressedDeflate) and put back
   Content-Encoding: deflate so the browser receives the original stream.

   Also added a short-circuit: if the compressed body itself exceeds
   maxBodyBytes, skip immediately rather than decompressing first.

2. Accept-Encoding: gzip, identity → gzip, deflate, identity
   Now that deflate is supported, advertise it so servers can use it
   (instead of only gzip) when serving to the proxy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add E2E tests for table filtering and sorting (#860)

`wiki-table` and its filter/sort components had no E2E coverage despite
being complex user-facing features with multiple interaction modes and
localStorage persistence.

## What's added

**`e2e/tests/table-filter-sort.spec.ts`** — 13 serial Playwright tests
using a dedicated 5-row test page (`Name | Category | Score`).

### Sorting
- Neutral `⇕` → ascending `↑` → descending `↓` → neutral cycle via
sort-arrow clicks
- Row order verified at each step (alphabetical A→Z / Z→A on `Name`)
- `sorted` CSS class and `aria-sort` attribute asserted on the active
`<th>`

### Filtering
- Checkbox popover opens on header click; unchecking "Vegetable" filters
to 3 rows, status bar shows `"3 of 5 rows"`
- Filter dot (`.filter-dot`) appears on filtered column header
- `.tag-filter-clear` pill restores all 5 rows
- Multi-column: Category=Fruit AND Name=Alpha-only → 1 row (`"1 of 5
rows"`)

### State persistence
- Sort and filter each survive a navigate-away → navigate-back cycle
(via `localStorage`)

### Keyboard accessibility
- Sort ascending, OK, and Cancel buttons are focusable and
Enter-activatable inside the popover

## Notable implementation detail

`tableRows()` helper scopes to `.table-wrapper tbody tr` to avoid
matching the hidden slotted source `<table>` that `wiki-table` keeps in
the light DOM. Playwright locator chaining pierces both shadow roots
(`wiki-table` → `table-filter-popover`) automatically.

`beforeEach` clears all `wiki-table-state:*` localStorage keys to ensure
each test starts from a known baseline.
